clues: This medication was granted accelerated approval in 1996 by the FDA, but was almost pulled from the market nearly 15 years later in 2010 as the manufacturer never completed required studies showing efficacy and safety. The medication remained on the market after public outcry almost exclusively as a generic and a phase 4 trial was finally completed in 2016
Because this medication activates alpha-adrenergic receptors, it can cause piloerection and an itchy or tingly scalp

This medication is completely inert after swallowing it to avoid aggressive vasoconstriction in the GI tract. Because of the systemic vasoconstriction, you have to avoid taking it about 4 hours before bedtime because of supine hypertension.

In 100 AD., Greek physician Aretaeus of Cappadocia, who also provided one of the first detailed clinical definitions of this disease, recommended treating symptoms with a mixture of owl's blood and wine. Because owls were associated with night vision and the ability to navigate through the air, he believed that their blood might transfer some traits to the human body

In the 1800s and early 1900s, European and US pharmacies sold factory rolled cigarettes containing plants like stramonium and belladona to treat this disease. These were surprisingly still on the market until the 1980s

Hippocrates was the first to realize that respiratory distress correlated directly with environmental triggers. He observed that fishermen, tailors, and metalworkers were especially prone to this disease

Evidence of this disease was found in a fossil belonging to a stem turtle from the Triassic period, which was roughly 240 million years ago. Evidence has also been found in other dinosaur fossils such as the Centrosaurus, a four legged horned dinosaur
More common in dogs than humans, this disease almost exclusively affects large dog breeds like Greyhounds and Great Danes, most commonly presenting in the legs
This is the oldest identifiable example of cancer in the human lineage. This bony malignancy was found in a 1.7 million old metatarsal of an early human ancestor

Carriers of recessive familial Mediterranean fever (FMF) mutations have natural immunity to this disease. At one point in history, Jewish citizens were blamed because they carried FMF mutations and died at lower rates than Christians

Many believe that the nursery rhyme 'Ring Around the Posy' is a reference to this disease where 'rosy' represents the red rash, 'posies' refers to the herbs carried to ward off the smell, and 'ashes' symbolizes mass deaths

While there are three major clinical forms of this disease, the most common presentation is sudden high fevers, terrible pains, and painful pus filled lymph nodes. The other two forms are septicemic and pneumonic.
Originally dubbed 'black measles' due to its distinct, dark purplish rash, this was first identified in the 1890s in Idaho

In 1922, the Governor of Montana lost both his daughter and his son-in-law to the disease. This motivated a massive influx of funding and research and Dr. Spencer of the US Public Health Service led a research team where he injected himself with a large dose of mashed wood ticks which actually worked as a vaccine

This disease is endemic to the Americas with cases concentrated in the US, Mexico, and Central and South America. Despite its name, this arthropod borne disease actually has the highest incidence in the Southeast and South-Central regions of the United States
